title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People's 2026 celebration is set for Saturday, June 6, featuring a parade and festival.
The parade starts at 11 a.m. in Copley Square, ending at Boston Common with events running until 8 p.m.
Mayor Michelle Wu supports the event, highlighting its focus on diversity and intersectionality of the LGBTQ+ community.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released per a federal judge's order and reunited with family.
The girl was reportedly mistaken as associated with gang members and transferred to New York before being released.
The Department of Homeland Security intends to pursue deportation; the girl's lawyer plans to seek legal residency for her.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are experiencing scoring struggles, threatening their Eastern Conference wild-card playoff spot.
Coach Marco Sturm is considering reshuffling the lineup to spark scoring, especially among top players like David Pastrnak.
The Bruins' power play has dropped to 14.3% conversion rate post-Olympics, compounding their scoring issues.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss student, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 for trauma survivor programs.
Cogne is a visiting neurorehabilitation researcher at Harvard Medical School focusing on trauma recovery and patient support.
The fundraiser will benefit the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women’s Hospital, highlighting the need for trauma research funding.

title: Fire destroys Newbury home, kills pets as family escapes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fire destroyed a Newbury home early Friday, killing a dog and cat while four residents escaped unhurt.
Firefighters responded to the blaze at 49 Fruit St. shortly before 1 a.m., requiring mutual aid from neighboring towns.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by local police and the Massachusetts State Police Fire and Explosion Investigation Unit.

title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Andrew Little founded the Cambridge Recipe Club to share local recipes and foster community interaction around Massachusetts-based ingredients.
The club, with over 1,000 members, offers a free weekly newsletter focusing on recipes suitable for the Boston area.
Community involvement includes shared recipes and collaborations with local chefs like Robert Harris of Season to Taste.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Live Nation employee Ben Baker's Slack messages mocked customers, saying they were 'so stupid' and boasted about 'robbing them blind'.
Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell continues antitrust suit against Live Nation, despite DOJ settlement, citing insufficient consumer protection.
DOJ settlement with Live Nation caps service fees and allows ticket distribution choice, but no admission of wrongdoing from the company.

title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey and Attorney General Andrea Campbell launched a portal for reporting ICE misconduct in Massachusetts.
The portal allows residents to submit reports, photos, and videos of alleged unlawful activities by federal immigration agents.
Campbell's office released an updated 'know your rights' guide with tips on handling ICE operations and legal resources.

title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA referees for rewarding flopping and foul-baiting during games.
Brown expressed frustration over officiating after losing to Oklahoma City and a prior ejection against the Spurs.
Despite frustrations, Brown scored 34 points and played 41 minutes in the close loss to the Thunder.

title: Oscars 2026 predictions: Full list of who will win each category
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien will host the 2026 Oscars on Sunday at 7 p.m. on ABC.
"One Battle After Another" is predicted to win Best Picture but faces competition from "Sinners".
Michael B. Jordan is expected to win Best Actor for his dual role in "Sinners".
